title: Chromogenic Behaviour of Eriochrome Cyanine R.C. in the Spectrophotometric Determination of Scandium, Yttrium and Lanthanum
author: Kailash N. Munshi
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Sulphohydroxydimethyl fuchsone dicarboxylic acid (Eriochrome Cyanine R.C.) has been described as a reagent for the spectrophotometric determination of scandium, yttrium and lanthanum. The red coloured chelates formed have a composition 1 : 2 (metal : reagent) in all the cases. The colour of the dye changes with change in  \(p\) H. The  \(\lambda\)   \(max\)   of scandium chelate is 535 m µ  (at  \(p\) H 5.0), of yttrium chelate is 530 m µ  (at  \(p\) H 6.0) and of lanthanum chelate 480 m µ  (at  \(p\) H 6.2). However the wavelength of study for lanthanum was selected at 500 m µ . A four fold excess of the reagent is required for full colour development. Other characteristics, i.e., range for adherence to Beer's law, range for effective photometric determination,  \(p\) H range of stability and molecular extinction coefficient have also been determined.
steps_raw: Sulphohydroxydimethyl fuchsone dicarboxylic acid (Eriochrome Cyanine R.C.) has been described as a reagent for the spectrophotometric determination of scandium, yttrium and lanthanum. The red coloured chelates formed have a composition 1 : 2 (metal : reagent) in all the cases. The colour of the dye changes with change in  \(p\) H. The  \(\lambda\)   \(max\)   of scandium chelate is 535 m µ  (at  \(p\) H 5.0), of yttrium chelate is 530 m µ  (at  \(p\) H 6.0) and of lanthanum chelate 480 m µ  (at  \(p\) H 6.2). However the wavelength of study for lanthanum was selected at 500 m µ . A four fold excess of the reagent is required for full colour development. Other characteristics, i.e., range for adherence to Beer's law, range for effective photometric determination,  \(p\) H range of stability and molecular extinction coefficient have also been determined.
